author: somnergy
content: If this works, that's good but why do it this way, that is copy part of zilkworm into a new repo ? Zilkworm already has support for EESTs and currently going through some structural changes and direct ere-benchmarks integration

author: somnergy
content: Sorry for the trouble, i made the commit and it'd be good to attribute credit - https://github.com/erigontech/zilkworm/commit/fbcf70cd6556b5b7842dd9c425e3d59a2836d6cf

author: developeruche
content:
It is majorly for spec compliance, the guest program input is passed as `ssz` encoded bytes.

This is the link to the particular spec in question.
https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/projects/zkevm/src/ethereum/forks/amsterdam/stateless_guest.py

author: developeruche
content: Zilkworkm was not copied but rather used as an library pulled during build time. I couldn't use the native `stf` Zilkworkm provided because it expects it input in RLP format and it input structure wasn't spec compatible, that was why i did another implementation of `stateless` using zilkworm as a lib. I think we can move the entire logic `core` introduces to `zilkworm` and use zilkworm as the library for the sp1 guest program rather that reimplementing another stf inside `ere-guest`, this is what you are drawing  my attention to right?

author: developeruche
content:
The part of zilkworm's codebase that was copied was the sp1 guest program which is called `guest_hypercube`, where everything that make the guest program work (e.g linker, runtime, entrypoint etc.) was copied aside the `main.cpp`, I did that because I needed to use these modules in `core` i created for spec compliance if you think it's cool we can move those modules to zilkworm's repo, so to create a spec compliant zilkwork sp1 guest all one would need to do is to import `zilkworm` as a lib. What is you take on this? 


I see zilkworm the way I see [reth-stateless](https://github.com/paradigmxyz/stateless), it think it would be great to have zilkwork guest program as a part of the [ere-guest](https://github.com/eth-act/ere-guests) programs

author: somnergy
content:
Spec - idk, for now I would treat whatever there is as one way to look at it.
It's fine like I said, if it is working for you - it's a blanket LGTM from my side on your PR 🙂

author: somnergy
content: You can spin up things as you wish of course. For upstream zilkworm, we are looking at faster performance for which this may not be apt. So, we are facilitating a bridge to run ere, this and Glamsterdam would be released soon (ish). So, you can see my reluctance in splitting attention

author: developeruche
content: Yeah... I see, that makes sense

author: somnergy
content: Comment on different format of input, again: we are making some fundamental changes and moving away from RLP (or ssz) towards flat objects (not serialized bytes deserialized, but objects themselves as structs). Our focus next is even more performance + easy to verify input making more validations cheaper

author: chfast
content: Glamsterdam is partly blocked by bal-devnet-7 or any other unreleased devnet tests

author: jsign
content: Nice, glad someone is experimenting with alternative input design. If at any point you end up writing something about it or have any link to code when available, I'm interested in understanding more. Mostly thinking on further iterations of the specs guest input design.
